feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAPFM06P - BADI_MM_PO_OC_EMAIL for S4CORE 107 and SAPSCRIPT
entry.content:
Dear all,

we are using standard SAPFM06P as program for email output of SAPSCRIPT forms out of purchase orders. By standard only partner function "VN" can be used as recipient, but we want to use a different partner function (which analogous is possible on SD side).
I found SAP note 217371 with BADI_MM_PO_OC_EMAIL and method CHANGE_RECIPIENTS which would basically facilitate our needs. Unfortunately this badi is only available for S4CORE up to 101 (we are using 107) and only for ADOBE (we are using SAPSCRIPT). I wasn`t able to find any other notes suggesting other solutions.

Do you know of any alternative badi/ solution available for our version and SAPSCRIPT

Thanks a lot and BR
Lukas

entry.title: SUM DMO with S/4HANA conversion
entry.content:
Hi,

In an upcoming project we have to bring a HCM system which is currently running on Windows and MSSQL to Linux and HANA. In addition to that the system should also be converted from ECC to S/4HANA. I looked up some information on DMO and only found a help article where this option is mentioned in connection with hyperscalers.

Does anybody have experience with this scenario and tell me if DMO with all those requirements is possible to do in an on-premise landscape?

 

Best regards,

Alex

entry.title: issue "Unexpected Error" with test run of SAP report RSIRPIRL
entry.content:
Hi,

We're getting the test run result "Unexpected error" after executing the SAP report RSIRPIRL with the following input entries:

 

Based on "SAP Note: 1773722", it says that such errors can be ignored as this entry is referring to be corrupted and there is no physical document assigned to this entry.

But how would we make sure that the report test run performed correctly? Why this report doesn't show test run results?

 

Regards

Reddy

entry.title: Critical Success Factors for a Seamless SAP S/4HANA Transition
entry.content:
For many companies, the move to SAP S/4HANA is a strategic step toward digital transformation. The benefits are clear—real-time analytics, streamlined processes, and a modern user interface. However, the transition often turns out to be more complex than initially expected.

From my experience with SAP S/4HANA projects, these are some of the most critical factors for success:

  1. Choosing the Right Migration Approach

The first major decision is the migration strategy. There is no one-size-fits-all approach, but selecting the right path early on is crucial:

  • Greenfield: A complete system redesign, ideal for companies looking to optimize processes but requiring significant change management.
  • Brownfield: A system conversion that retains existing structures, making the transition smoother but limiting flexibility.
  • Selective Data Transition: A hybrid approach that allows companies to retain critical historical data while restructuring processes where needed.

Many companies struggle with this choice. What has worked best in your experience?

  1. Process Harmonization and the Role of SAP Standard

An SAP S/4HANA project is more than just a technical migration—it is an opportunity to rethink processes. A key question in every project is:

  • How much standardization is feasible?
  • Which custom developments are truly necessary?

The closer companies stay to the SAP standard, the easier future upgrades become, and the lower the long-term maintenance effort. However, business-specific requirements must also be carefully considered.

  1. Data Migration – A Critical Success Factor

Data quality often becomes a major roadblock in SAP S/4HANA projects. Missing, duplicate, or inconsistent data can lead to significant delays and operational disruptions. A well-defined data cleansing strategy and thorough test migrations are essential to ensure a smooth go-live.

  1. Change Management and Training

A successful transition to SAP S/4HANA is not just about technology—it’s also about people. Many companies underestimate the impact of change on end users. The most successful projects focus on:

  • Early communication about what is changing and why
  • Comprehensive training programs for key users and end users
  • Post-go-live support to address issues quickly and ensure adoption

Without a structured change management approach, even the most technically sound implementation can face resistance from users.

  1. Leveraging SAP BTP and Automation

SAP S/4HANA provides a solid foundation, but many companies look beyond the core system to drive additional value. The SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) enables:

  • Fiori applications for an improved user experience
  • AI-driven automation to reduce manual effort
  • Cloud and hybrid integration through the SAP Integration Suite

The challenge is to determine which innovations truly add value without adding unnecessary complexity.

Final Thoughts

Moving to SAP S/4HANA is not just an IT project—it is a business transformation. Companies that carefully evaluate their migration strategy, focus on data quality, embrace standardization where possible, and invest in change management are the ones that achieve long-term success.

What have been your biggest challenges in SAP S/4HANA projects? How did you approach them? I’d love to hear your insights.

entry.title: In IFRS16, post the total value of the contract to the standard liability
entry.content:
Good morning
In the IFRS16 process we are using the direct entry in the balance sheet for the standard, with this making the entries in the ASSET, DEPRECIATION COMPENSATION and INTEREST account according to the IFRS16 standard, our client wants to standard the entry of the CONTRACT VALUE in a LIABILITY account, could you help us?

entry.title: SAP standard library to support excel data read
entry.content:
Could you kindly confirm whether Ican use the NPM XLS library to upload and read XLS data, and if it is safe to do so in use SAP UI5 Application.


Additionally, I would appreciate it if you could suggest any alternative solutions for this task without NPM(third party API).


Thank you for your assistance.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: "Revolutionizing SAP Implementations: How AI is Changing the Game! 🚀"
entry.content:
Leveraging AI in SAP S/4HANA Implementations: A Game Changer?

Introduction

AI is rapidly transforming industries, but how does it fit into SAP S/4HANA implementations? While AI adoption in SAP environments is still low—only 5% of organizations currently use AI and ML—the potential is massive. With SAP Joule introducing 140 AI agents (and planning 400 by 2025), the focus is on improving processes and reducing inefficiencies.

Let's explore how AI can streamline requirement gathering, enhance blueprinting, and optimize the overall SAP implementation process.

 

The Current Landscape: AI in SAP Environments

Organizations still rely on traditional business processes, with SAP use cases focused on data warehousing, self-service analytics, and data discovery. However, integrating SAP data with non-SAP data remains a major challenge for achieving robust AI/ML models. Key barriers include:

  • Limited system access: Incomplete access to source and target systems creates bottlenecks.

  • Software usability issues: High complexity often leads to inefficiencies.

  • Data culture gaps: Organizations need to retrain and hire talent to harness AI’s full potential.

AI can bridge these gaps by enhancing usability and connectivity while reducing the dependency on deep technical expertise.

 

AI in SAP Implementation: Where It Fits

One of the most promising applications of AI in SAP S/4HANA projects is in the requirement gathering and blueprinting phase. Traditionally, this process is time-consuming and involves extensive coordination. AI-powered tools can revolutionize this by:

✅ Capturing and consolidating user requirements automatically
✅ Generating accurate documentation with minimal manual input
✅ Identifying process gaps for testing and training
✅ Reducing misalignment and inefficiencies in design and build phases

AI co-pilots and accelerators are already proving effective in recording user inputs, analyzing data, and structuring blueprints—allowing experts to validate and refine instead of starting from scratch.

 

How AI Streamlines SAP Implementation: A Step-by-Step Flow

1️⃣ AI-Powered Requirement Gathering:
AI captures existing processes and user pain points automatically, reducing reliance on meetings and manual inputs.

2️⃣ Intelligent Blueprinting:
AI-generated documents provide structured insights, identifying key requirements, business rules, and potential process optimizations.

3️⃣ Enhanced Testing & Training:
Gap documents generated by AI facilitate seamless testing, change management, and user training.

4️⃣ Optimized Implementation & Deployment:
With AI-driven documentation, project teams spend less time on redundant tasks, leading to faster go-lives and improved accuracy.

 

Interactive Discussion: AI in SAP Implementations

Q: How can AI-powered tools be used to capture, consolidate, and analyze user requirements and pain points before the design and build phases?

✅ AI accelerators can record user inputs on current processes and requirements, reducing the need for extensive coordination and meetings.
✅ AI consolidates these inputs and generates gap documents, essential for testing and training purposes.
✅ AI-generated documents are reviewed and validated by experts, leading to more accurate and efficient design solutions.
✅ Using AI in this way significantly reduces the time and effort required for requirement gathering and blueprinting, improving implementation quality.

 

Q: How can AI help address challenges in SAP implementations, such as incomplete access to source and target systems, software usability issues, and high complexity?

✅ AI improves ease of use and connectivity, reducing the need for technical skills.
✅ AI co-pilots help bridge the gap between user expectations and SAP reality.
✅ AI enhances connectivity to various source and target systems.
✅ AI improves the ability of technology to meet different use case requirements.

 

Future Outlook: The AI-Driven SAP Revolution

With AI’s role in SAP expanding, organizations that embrace AI-powered accelerators will gain a competitive edge in SAP S/4HANA transformations. While current AI tools focus on process improvement, the future lies in AI’s ability to automate and optimize the entire SAP implementation lifecycle.

💡 What’s Next?

  • More AI-driven automation in testing, training, and deployment

  • Improved integration of SAP and non-SAP data for better AI models

  • Expansion of AI agents in SAP Joule to enhance usability and decision-making

AI in SAP S/4HANA is no longer just an idea—it’s happening now. The question is: Are we ready to leverage it to its full potential?

entry.title: Enhance MB21
entry.content:
When creating a Reservation to transfer materials from one Plant to another, I want the WBS Element field to be positioned next to materials, so that each Material has its own corresponding WBS Element in the same row.

Currently, the WBS Element appears as a single field at the top, but I need it to be linked to each Material individually in the same row.

How can I achieve this?

entry.title: Overloaded - Load not able to complete GI(OUTB)
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We have an issue where the load is overloaded for the inbound delivery (5931362) EWM-1379.Please need your support to rectify the issue.

regards

Shiva

entry.title: S4H Upg Simplification SI22 Generic SDMI code 12
entry.content:
started with upgrade prerequisite in HBX system.

with all Snotes implementation

Steps to reproduce subjected issue:

execute transaction SE38 : report : /SDF/RC_START_CHECK

execute

entry.title: Cannot edit Analytical KPI (Smart Business) tile in Fiori Launchpad Designer
entry.content:
Hello,

We have a problem with some of the tiles in our Fiori Launchpad Designer, when trying to edit a composite tile such as F3382 or F3383 it does not allow us, is it a problem with our system or if by SAP standard a composite tile cannot be modified?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: SAP S4 HANA New O/P management replace idocs with APIs
entry.content:
Hi,

We have our current solution based on idocs, where in we send the billing documents from SAP to third party system. Biztalk is the middleware, which transforms the idoc details and sends it in XML format.

We are exploring the options of replacing the idocs with API and check the feasibility of using new S4 HANA new output management.

Has anyone explored this option ? Kindly share your thoughts.

Thanks

entry.title: IFRS16 Solution for Defer Interest Posting
entry.content:
Hi

For IFRS16 figure has the following figure: Contract Value THB 200,000, Present Value THB 180,000.

Standard SAP will capitalized asset by posting

Dr. ROU Asset 180,000

  Cr. Asset Clearing 180,000

Dr. Asset Clearing 180,000

  Cr. Lease Liability 180,000

If my client wants to have additional posting for Defer Interest with the following entry: -

Dr. Defer Interest 20,000

  Cr. Lease Liability 20,000

Is there any BADI to insert the following entry upon posting to FI.

Thank you.

Kai

entry.title: How to Download & install (offline) SAP for the Practice only.
entry.content:
Hi,

I am a student and I want to be do the practices at home in the SAP FICO & SD Module but I have found same error from server provider it was charged too high cost for the practices and server is very slow, so today I am finally think that I should practices in offline server, hence I requesting to all sap user & expert please suggest a batter way for the same.

Please guide me how to install SAP offline in my PC please share any link, video or full setup for the above mention query.

Hope you all will give me a great solution for the same

Regards,

Sonam Singh

entry.title: T.code CJ8G acquisition value negative in depreciation area 15
entry.content: T.code CJ8G acquisition value negative in depreciation area 15

entry.title: In Private Cloud, is there sub order function? How to realize muli-service orders for one SO line?
entry.content:
Our system will be upgraded from ECC6 to Private Cloud in the coming days, and we would like to check the standard solution for concurrent business, means product and service(Startup) and service contract(extended warranty) sales together for transaction business.

If the Sales Order line is service(Startup), can it trigger multi-service orders (maybe sub order?) generation for the same sales order line? and the revenue recognition be realized for the accordingly value at every service order completed?

For example:

Sales Order

Item 10   Product                               10 qty ,   Unit Price   1000 RMB,    Total Value  10000 RMB

Item 20   Install Service                     10 qty,    Unit Price      10 RMB,     Total Value  100 RMB

Item 20 is the service for item 10 Product, these products are delivered to Customer at 2024.02.10, and billing is created for both item 10 and 20 based on delivery, and when Customer received the product, they would like to install these products in three times in different date:

2024.02.15 Provide Install service 2 qty, when service is completed, need realize Revenue Recognition 2*10=20 RMB

2024.03.10 Provide Install service 3 qty, when service is completed, need realize Revenue Recognition 3*10=30 RMB

2024.04.05 Provide Install service 5 qty, when service is completed, need realize Revenue Recognition 5*10=50 RMB

is that possible? 

Thank you!

CZ

entry.title: Supplier Hierarchy in S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Hello fellow members,

Is Supplier Hierarchy available in S/4 HANA On=Prem or Cloud edition. I see the SPRO config under MM->Purchasing-->Supplier Master but do not find any Transaction code to create and maintain the Supplier Hierarchy nodes in front end.

When I check the SAP Help, it takes me to Supplier Hierarchy under Retail & Fashion.

Is there a Transaction code in MM similar to VDH1N for Customer Hierarchy?

Any help in this regards is highly appreciated!

Thanks,

Aakash

entry.title: GR activity is falling on weekend
entry.content: GR activity of production order is falling on weekend because of 7 days plant factory calendar. Requirement is that GR activity should not happen on weekend without changing the plant factory calendar to 5 days. In PPDS this is possible by assigning a 5 days calendar to handling resource but in S/4 side it refers to plant factory calendar and material is available on weekend. The 7 days plant calendar has been set to enable Ad-hoc order execution on weekend.

entry.title: ERROR: Enter a warehouse process type/SCWM/L35
entry.content:
Scenario - Planned replenishment with POSC LOSC steps. 

Configured POSC with two steps - step 1 move replenishment quantity to interim repacking area, Step 2 move the quantity to final replenishment storage bin

combined with LOSC - to move replenishment quantity from interim repack area to level 2 storage area, and then move from level 2 storage area to final replenishment storage bin. 

First WT got triggered from the picking storage type to interim packing area as per the POSC setup after running planned replenishment using t-code /SCWM/REPL.

After HU is completed at the interim packing area work center - next WT creation as per LOSC is failing due to the following error

"Enter a warehouse process type    /SCWM/L3    5"

WPT is configures as part of POSC/LOSC setup but still system isn"t able to pick it up.

entry.title: Reduce TR import runtime
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,

 

We are using CALM to deploy TR's in our S/4HANA landscape. Currently there are around 700 Features created and it is taking 6 to 7 days to complete the deployment in each system in our landscape.

 

Recently, we built the system for dress rehearsal 2 , which took 6 days to deploy all features and associated TR's. But we in worry how mange in Prod environment as we will not get that long downtime to just for import the TR's.

 

We would request your help to know if we have any option to deploy multiple features (ie. Import all) or any other way we can deploy TR's in mass from CALM.

 

Thank you in advance

 

Regards,

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.
